channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Litter, 4th July
item.description:
Litter has been dumped all along burnt ash lane

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Burnt Ash Lane, Plaistow, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Car blocking other cars to go away, 4th July
item.description:
All cars are always parked so badly in burleigh way, lot of them parking just to take a coffee or a tan

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Church Street, Enfield
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Car Stopped and disposed of cartons of laughing gas, 4th July
item.description:
Car stopped. Bloke got out and disposed of boxes into the bush and stream. Looked at what was thrown and it is boxes and canisters of laughing gas. Number plate contains a 67. Guy had long hair.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 44, Longmeadow Road, Longlands, Sidcup, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: More fly tipping, 4th July
item.description:
People seem to be ignoring the sign.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Glading Terrace, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Forest bike, 4th July
item.description:
Blocking pavement

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Norwood Road, Dulwich Village, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Lime bike, 4th July
item.description:
Should not be on this path

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 4, Burbage Road, Dulwich Village, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Weeds on the road, 4th July
item.description:
Please see the photo.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Rectory Lane, Albany Park, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: All the public trees need to be trimmed and all the weed removed, 4th July
item.description:
Streets need to be trimmed and weeds removed as they are blocking the view when you need to cross the road making very dangerous.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Sundridge Avenue, Elmstead, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly tipping problem getting worse, 4th July
item.description:
Issue with fly tipping on this road for months, always gradually builds up

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Brewery Road, Plumstead
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Street need to be seeep, 4th July
item.description:
Sundridge ave street are full of rubbish, leaves and broken tree branches. Street has not been swept I'm weeks. Streets need to be cleaned and maintained

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Sundridge Avenue, Elmstead, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Household Ru bish, 4th July
item.description:
Irresponsible and uncaring people continue to use Penge East Dry Cleaner's shop front as a dumping site for their rubbish, on a nightly basis. It is so selfish and it's obvious these people have no pride in how our local streets look. Can CCTV cameras be placed on nearby lamp posts to capture these serial dumpers?

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Venner Road, Penge, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Nails on road, 4th July
item.description:
Lots of Nails are on the main DFS round about when approaching from Mottingham
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Yorkshire Grey Roundabout, Middle Park, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Stretch of Green Chain Walk encroached upon., 4th July
item.description:
A garden at BR7 6NG has been extended at the back, enclosing a significant area of Green Chain Walk. At least one tree has been removed, and the remaining area has become a bit of a rubbish tip. The fencing was put up maybe 2 years ago.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Cow Path, Elmstead, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dead fox in visitor bay corner of Alcock Crescent, 4th July
item.description:
Since last night

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Alcock Crescent, Barnes Cray, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Plywood and cardboard dumped, 4th July
item.description:
Plywood and cardboard dumped by fence on Rochester Way close to bus stop U

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Rochester Way, Eltham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: The tree was removed in 2025 and a stake put in to mark the spot, 4th July
item.description:
The stake has been removed. I think it wasn't very secure in the ground. I noticed it had gone on 14 June 2026. I wondered if it meant the tree was about to be replaced but this has not happened yet. I do hope the tree will be replaced or until then the stake be replaced to mark where the tree will go.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Sidney Road, Elmers End, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dead fox outside building entrance, 4th July
item.description:
There is the body and head of a fox lying outside the entrance to our building, directly in front of the entrance doors. It appears to be dead and should be removed as soon as possible, as it is unpleasant and could pose a health risk. Could you please arrange for it to be removed? Thank you.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Quebec Way, Rotherhithe, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Pavement slab sinking, 4th July
item.description:
Outside Pizza Hut there is a dangerous trip hazard. One side of a paving slab has no support underneath and if someone stands on it, it tilts creating trip hazard.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 10-12, London Road, Forest Hill, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Pavement Defect (uneven surface / cracked paving slab)

item.title: Overgrown vegetation, 4th July
item.description:
The one way arrow sign and the chevron sign on the Crittals corner roundabout near the traffic light under the Sidcup by pass are hidden behind overgrown trees. And the traffic lights opposite (as you come down off the A20 on the B&Q side) are getting obscured by trees.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Crittall's Corner, North Cray, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dumped sheet steel, 4th July
item.description:
Entrance to Forest Hill station car park. There used to be this sheet of steel covering the hole in the ground at the entrance. The hole has been repaired but the steel no taken away. It is approx 3m by 1.2m and very heavy. It is slowly being propelled over the footway and into the road. It will likely cause an accident soon. I reported this several weeks ago and nothing was done.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Perry Vale, Forest Hill, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
